articles.title: Scientists discover hidden gut-brain circuit that triggers protein cravings
articles.description: When the body runs low on protein, the gut sends powerful signals to the brain that reshape cravings and push animals to seek essential amino acids instead of sugar. Researchers say this newly discovered gut-brain network could transform our understanding of appetite, nutrition, and obesity.

articles.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
articles.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

articles.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
articles.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

articles.title: Letters, May 23, 2026: ‘Too many people in the national parks’
articles.description: Popularity problem There’s a problem in Canada’s national parks and that problem is there are too many people visiting the most popular places. So what do the sages of this country do? They offer free admission to the parks from June 19 until September 7. Yep, that will solve the problem. J.D. ROUND

articles.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
articles.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

articles.title: K’ómoks Treaty Act passes in B.C. legislature
articles.description: The K’ómoks Treaty Act, which pertains to approximately 3,400 hectares of land on Vancouver Island, was passed in B.C. legislature today.

articles.title: B.C. woman returns home after 2 months in induced coma in China
articles.description: The daughter of a British Columbia woman who slipped into a coma in China while visiting her family says her mom has returned home after a marathon journey.

articles.title: Look inside Oakridge Park: Vancouver’s new luxury mall opens to huge crowds
articles.description: "Vancouver is finally catching up," one shopper said. "My father is from Turkey, where malls of this scale with high-rise apartments above them are normal."

articles.title: stal̕əw̓asəm Bridge closures this weekend
articles.description: The stal̕əw̓asəm Bridge will be closing again this upcoming weekend. From Friday, May 29, at 9 p.m. until Monday, June 1, at 5 a.m., the bridge will be closed in both directions.
